name: Icy Veins
author: Icy Veins guide writers
methodology: Broad-meta tier lists per role and bracket, aimed at the general player population.

name: Method
author: Tactyks (M+); Method raiders (raid)
methodology: Top-end lens: M+ list by Tactyks, raid list written with Method raiders from a race-to-world-first output perspective.
notes: Raid list omits Vengeance DH (not ranked on the page). Method's list content is unchanged since its 2026-03-31 revision; re-verified live and era-confirmed (Devourer present) 2026-07-09.

name: Wowhead
author: Wowhead class writers
methodology: Rankings for all classes based on Raid and Mythic+ performance; also the primary PTR datamining source for 12.1 class tuning and tier-set changes.

name: Archon
author: Archon.gg (statistical)
methodology: Statistical tier lists computed from Warcraft Logs parses and Blizzard leaderboards — 14-day rolling window, refreshed daily. Raid tiers read from the throughput metric tier list (NOT the default popularity grouping); M+ tiers from the M+ score tier list. Mirrored at u.gg/wow.
notes: Fetch recipe: parse the __NEXT_DATA__ JSON script tag from raw HTML (WebFetch markdown drops it). Raid pages carry three tierLists (popularity/throughput/survivability) — use metric "throughput"; M+ pages use metric "score". Mirrored at u.gg/wow.

name: WoWMeta
author: Hypermeta Network (algorithmic)
methodology: Algorithmic S/A/B/C/D tier lists: Ckmeans clustering of the lower-bound 95% CI of official Blizzard M+ scores per spec (raid and PvP lists too). Population-statistics lens — distinct from Archon (Warcraft Logs top-end) and editorial lists. Updated frequently.
notes: JS-rendered (SvelteKit) — WebFetch returns an empty shell; fetch via the r.jina.ai proxy. Scale S/A/B/C/D plus 'X = Unranked' (X is omitted, never stored). Raid pages verified Midnight-era but hold 0-2 records as of 2026-07-09 (nearly all specs still X) — ingest raid tiers when they populate.

name: Warcraft Logs
author: Warcraft Logs aggregate statistics
methodology: Per-spec aggregate combat-log statistics: median (50th percentile) rDPS/HPS and parse counts. Live Season 1 raid = zone 46 (Mythic = difficulty 5, size 20, partition 3 = patch 12.0.7); M+ Season 1 = zone 47 (difficulty 10, size 5, partition 1); zone 54 is the 12.1 PTR raid and zone 56 is the 12.1 PTR M+ ("Mythic+ Season 2 (PTR)", same recipe as zone 47) — both PTR-quality, era-tagged "ptr", kept out of live baselines. Sanctioned long-term access is the v2 GraphQL API (free OAuth client, exposes live zones only — PTR zones are HTML-only); HTML statistics tables are the fallback, fetched fresh every run (policy 2026-07-08: no at-most-daily cap; datacenter IPs are Cloudflare-blocked on the HTML endpoint so the runner uses the API).

pages.label: 12.1 PTR M+ testing (zone 56, Mythic+ Season 2 PTR)

name: Murlok.io
author: Murlok.io (Blizzard API leaderboard data)
methodology: Average M+ rating of each spec's own top-50 rated players (US/EU/KR/TW, refreshed ~8h). This is a top-end ceiling measure — NOT representation share and NOT a strength tier; every spec always samples exactly 50 players. Never convert to letter grades.

name: Bloodmallet (SimC)
author: Bloodmallet / SimulationCraft (tier MID1)
methodology: SimulationCraft per-spec simulations, refreshed ~daily. Target-count scaling (talent_target_scaling chart: DPS at 1/2/3/…/15 targets, best build per count) powers the fight-type profiles for DPS specs. Sim DPS is point-in-time and build-dependent — display as numbers, never letters. JSON API: bloodmallet.com/chart/get/{chart}/{fight_style}/{snake_case_class}/{spec}.

name: Mythicstats
author: raptoranka
methodology: Weekly per-spec M+ meta statistics from the top 2000 keys of each reset period: representation % per spec (the meta-share axis Murlok cannot provide), spec diversity by key level, top comps. JS-heavy — fetch via r.jina.ai proxy.

name: Blizzard 12.1 PTR notes
author: Blizzard (Linxy) — official PTR development notes
methodology: Canonical source for 12.1 tuning: the 'Midnight: Curse of Ula'tek PTR Development Notes' forum thread. Each PTR build's changes arrive as a new reply post (~weekly). Machine-readable via Discourse JSON (append .json). New waves are discovered via the Wowhead news RSS (exact pubDates), filtered for '12.1 PTR' + Development Notes/Class Tuning/Datamined. A new patch cycle means a NEW thread — re-discover per patch.

name: Community
author: Class Discords + verified creators (data/community.json)
methodology: Qualitative layer: 13 class Discords (links only — content not fetchable, auth + TOS) and 1-2 verified creators per class whose video transcripts feed cited creator takes (data/creator-takes.json). Opinion, never tier data.

pages.label: Verified creators → takes in spec drawers

name: SimulationCraft
author: SimC project nightly (engine-official)
methodology: Official nightly MID1_Raid Patchwerk report: one fixed high-ilvl profile per spec/hero-variant, iterations=7500 — we ingest the best variant per DPS spec as 'SimC nightly Patchwerk DPS'. Pure single-target, fixed gear: a sim baseline, not a rankings verdict (the project itself disclaims class comparisons — keep it a number, never a tier).
notes: Parse the .txt variant (plain text ranking of ~49 MID1_{Class}_{Spec}_{HeroVariant} profiles). Filter to DPS-role roster specs (tanks appear at low DPS; healers absent; Augmentation has no profile — unsimmable support spec).

name: Raidbots
methodology: User-run cloud SimC (Top Gear, Droptimizer, Quick Sim) — the canonical place to sim YOUR character. No public per-spec aggregates (re-verified 2026-07-01), so reference only.

name: QE Live
methodology: Voulk's healer gearing/trinket evaluation tool (all healer specs). Client-side SPA — no fetchable data, but actively maintained for Midnight (verified via its GitHub repo, 12.0.7 branches). Reference for the healer audience; the blog publishes dated Midnight healer articles (e.g. S1 embellishment tier list).

name: Liquid Armory
methodology: SimulationCraft-driven guild-simmed trinket and best-in-slot data. A gearing axis, not a spec-strength axis — linked as an external reference, never rendered as a tier column.
